title: pellaea andromedifolia f. pubescens
intro: Pellaea andromedifolia f. pubescens, also known as the woolly mountain fern, is a fern species in the family Pteridaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Americas, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pellaea andromedifolia f. pubescens include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made by drying the fronds and then steeping them in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
